content: Toolchain version specs in [toolchain] accept either a bare prefix-match string ('1.1' matches 1.1.x) or operator forms (>=, >, <=, <, =, ==) compared via lexicographic numeric segments. compareSemver returns null for non-numeric inputs so versionMatches falls back to literal/prefix matching. extractVersionToken pulls the first dotted-numeric run from --version output, so 'Python 3.12.1', 'v20.10.0', and 'git version 2.43.0' all parse correctly.

name: expand-toolchain-bin-dirs
description: expandToolchainBinDirs(paths) returns dirname(path) plus dirname(realpath(path)) for each binary, deduped in input order. The PATH-visible dir lets bare-name lookup succeed inside the sandbox; the realpath ancestor admits the read-after-symlink that follows. Required for symlinked installs like ~/.local/bin/claude → ~/.local/share/claude/versions/X.Y.Z. Result feeds both SandboxProfile.toolchainPaths (mount/allow) and the prepended sandbox PATH.
